cap_space_post_fa: TBD — significant commitments made (~$211M in secondary alone; precise post-FA figure pending official contract filings)

key_departures.impact: Placed on Reserve/Retired list on March 7, 2026. Was under contract at $8.67M cap hit for 2026, retirement saved the Rams ~$7.5M in cap space. Had been a starter but saw snap count drop from 70% in 2024 to 40% in 2025. His exit compounded the already critical CB need — he was one of only three CBs under contract entering the offseason (alongside Emmanuel Forbes and Josh Wallace).

Retired in early February 2026 after 11 seasons, all with the Rams. Was the last remaining player from the St. Louis era and the team's longest-tenured player before Higbee. His retirement opens a vacancy at right tackle.

Started 15 games in 2025, tied for the team lead with 3 INTs. A 2022 fourth-round pick who played outside and slot. Was a pending UFA; no reported re-signing through March 11. His departure (if confirmed) removes a quality starter from the CB room, though the McDuffie/Watson additions effectively replace — and upgrade — his role.
Missed most of 2025 season with multiple clavicle injuries. Was a pending UFA entering free agency. Departure expected and largely absorbed by new CB additions.
Acquired mid-season 2025 via trade with the Titans for a Day 3 pick swap. Played in the playoffs. Was a pending UFA. No confirmed re-signing through March 11.

Signed a 1-year, $10M fully guaranteed deal in 2025 but caught only 6 passes in 10 games. The Rams pivoted to heavy 13-personnel usage, reducing WR3 value. Not expected to be re-signed. Loss is minimal given reduced role and the Rams' shift away from 11 personnel.

Served as backup QB to Matthew Stafford in 2025; played in Week 18. Was a pending UFA. Was reportedly of interest to the Arizona Cardinals as a potential bridge starter. Loss leaves a backup QB need, though Stetson Bennett remains under contract.
Pending UFA. Depth corner. Departure is depth-level loss only, absorbed by new CB signings.
Veteran long-snapper brought back in November 2025 after Alex Ward was released mid-season. At 37, not a long-term solution — replaced by Joe Cardona signing.

signings.trade_details: Rams sent 2026 1st-round pick (No. 29 overall), 2026 5th-round pick, 2026 6th-round pick, and 2027 3rd-round pick to Kansas City
signings.analysis: The marquee move of the Rams' offseason and the single biggest CB need-filler imaginable. McDuffie is a two-time All-Pro, 25 years old, and now the highest-paid cornerback in NFL history at $31M/year with $100M guaranteed — setting an NFL record for the position. The Rams surrendered four picks including their No. 29 first-rounder. McDuffie can play outside or slot, allowing the Rams to shadow elite WRs like Seattle's Jaxon Smith-Njigba. CB goes from critical Tier 1 need to a fully addressed position.

Watson follows his former Chiefs teammate McDuffie to LA, forming a formidable CB duo. At $17M AAV with $34M guaranteed, this is a legitimate starter-level contract at a position of great need. Watson was PFF-ranked top 20 among CBs last season. Contract structure has a clear out after Year 2 (only $7M cap hit in Year 1), but the guarantees signal real commitment. Paired with McDuffie, Emmanuel Forbes, and Quentin Lake (deployed as slot CB), the Rams' CB room transforms from the biggest weakness on the team to a genuine strength.

contract.notes: Maximum value $39M; guaranteed amount not yet fully reported

signings.analysis: Curl was coming off the best season of his career — 122 tackles, 2 regular-season INTs, 1 playoff INT that helped send the Rams to the NFC Championship. Re-signing him to 3 years/$36M was a priority, and the Rams delivered. At $12M AAV he's solidly in the starter tier for safety. Paired with the already-signed Quentin Lake (3-year extension with $25.7M guaranteed in January), the Rams' safety room is locked up long-term. S need fully resolved.

contract.notes: Worth up to $8M per reports; guaranteed amount not fully disclosed

signings.analysis: Higbee returns for his 11th season, keeping the Rams' 13-personnel (three-TE) identity intact. He had 25 catches for 281 yards and 3 TDs in 10 games in 2025. At $4M AAV on a 2-year deal, this is a veteran-minimum-adjacent contract reflecting his age (33) and injury history (missed 7 games in 2025, 14 in 2024). The Rams' TE room — Higbee, Colby Parkinson, Davis Allen, Terrance Ferguson — is deep and healthy. TE was not a critical need entering FA; re-signing Higbee maintains depth but the deal's low value doesn't suggest Higbee is a long-term fixture.

contract.notes: Two-year contract; financial terms not disclosed

signings.analysis: The Rams had a glaring special teams problem in 2025 — a muffed punt in the NFC Championship, along with mid-season special teams chaos (released kicker Joshua Karty and long-snapper Alex Ward). Cardona is a two-time Super Bowl champion long-snapper with 10 years of Patriots experience. A two-year deal provides genuine stability. LS need fully resolved under new special teams coordinator Bubba Ventrone.

Quessenberry was the Rams' first offseason signing, inking a 1-year, $1.5M deal to return as the swing tackle. Rob Havenstein's retirement created a legitimate starting OT vacancy, and Quessenberry does NOT solve that. He's a veteran backup/depth piece competing for a roster spot. OT remains a meaningful need at the starter level.

contract.notes: Exclusive rights free agent tender at league minimum

signings.analysis: Mevis was tendered as an ERFA, keeping him locked to the Rams for essentially free. The Rams' kicker situation was a disaster in 2025 (released Joshua Karty mid-season), so stabilizing it with a young kicker on a minimum deal makes sense. However, a minimum ERFA tender isn't a long-term solution. Mevis gets a chance to be the primary kicker in 2026.

contract.notes: Exclusive rights free agent tender at league minimum

signings.analysis: Dedich is retained as interior OL depth on a minimum tender. He's been a solid backup. The Rams' starting IOL (Steve Avila, Kevin Dotson) is intact but both enter the final year of their contracts, making this a position to monitor. Dedich's tender is low-cost depth maintenance.

contract.notes: Exclusive rights free agent tender at league minimum

signings.analysis: Smith was a return specialist and occasional WR contributor in 2025. The muffed punt in the NFC Championship was his, but he also made plays on offense. Retained at minimum cost. No impact on WR need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: The Rams executed the most comprehensive CB room overhaul in recent memory. Trading for Trent McDuffie (4yr/$124M, $100M gtd — highest-paid CB in NFL history) and signing Jaylen Watson (3yr/$51M, $34M gtd) transforms CB from the team's biggest need to a genuine strength. With Emmanuel Forbes (14 starts in 2025), Quentin Lake (slot CB/safety hybrid), and Kamren Kinchens also retained, the Rams now have 5-6 legitimate DB starters. CB is not a draft need. At most a Day 3 developmental pick for long-term depth.

Kamren Curl re-signed on a 3yr/$36M deal and Quentin Lake had already signed a 3-year extension with $25.7M guaranteed in January. Kamren Kinchens is also under contract. Safety is completely off the board. Do not draft here.

Rob Havenstein's retirement (February 2026) creates a genuine starting OT hole. Quessenberry re-signed on a 1yr/$1.5M swing-tackle deal — he is not the answer. The Rams also still have DJ Humphries listed as a UFA. OT remains a Tier 2 need. Strong candidate for a draft selection (Rounds 1-3) or another FA signing. The Rams' willingness to use their No. 13 overall pick here shouldn't be discounted.

Jimmy Garoppolo's departure (UFA, reportedly headed to Arizona) leaves Stetson Bennett as the only backup behind Matthew Stafford. Bennett is in the final year of his rookie deal. The Rams have shown interest in a backup QB solution. This is a Tier 3 need — not an emergency (Stafford is healthy) but a real vulnerability. Strong candidate for a mid-round draft pick (round 2-4) or a veteran free agent signing.

Tutu Atwell is expected to depart (no reports of a deal), but the Rams' shift to 13-personnel (three TEs) in 2025 reduced WR3 importance. Davante Adams (2yr, re-signed in 2025) and Puka Nacua are the top two; Nacua enters the final year of his rookie deal and will need an extension conversation. Jordan Whittington provides WR3/slot depth. WR remains a Tier 3 need — a complementary piece would be welcome but it's not urgent.

No EDGE moves in free agency. Byron Young (12 sacks in 2025) and Jared Verse (7.5 sacks) remain the starters. Young enters his final rookie contract year and is eligible for an extension; Verse is a season away from extension eligibility. Josaiah Stewart is a young developmental piece. No additions or subtractions — EDGE need (adding depth/succession planning) unchanged.

Steve Avila and Kevin Dotson both enter their final contract years in 2026, creating future IOL uncertainty. Dedich retained on ERFA minimum. No starter-level IOL signing in FA. Need remains for depth and succession planning — particularly at center if the Rams need to choose between Avila or Dotson long-term.

Higbee's re-signing (2yr/up to $8M) plus the retained foursome of Parkinson, Allen, and Ferguson gives the Rams one of the deepest TE rooms in the NFL. McVay has leaned heavily into 13-personnel and that infrastructure is fully intact. TE is not a draft priority. At most a late-round developmental pick.

No IDL moves in free agency. Kobie Turner, Poona Ford, and Braden Fiske anchor the interior. The group is solid but not elite as pass-rushers. No changes from FA — IDL remains a moderate need for rotational depth.

Nate Landman (re-signed mid-2025 season) is under contract. Troy Reeder was a UFA but was a depth player. No moves here. LB is not a significant need — Landman is a solid starter and the Rams have other, more pressing concerns.

key_additions.headline: Two-time All-Pro, 25, becomes NFL's highest-paid CB in blockbuster trade costing four draft picks including No. 29
McDuffie's former Chiefs teammate joins LA on starter deal, completing the most aggressive CB room overhaul of the 2026 offseason
Breakout safety retained after career year — 122 tackles, 3 combined INTs including a playoff pick — locking up a complete safety tandem with Quentin Lake
Franchise's all-time leading TE returns, preserving McVay's 13-personnel identity with all four TEs intact
Two-time Super Bowl champion long-snapper addresses one of the Rams' most glaring 2025 weaknesses on special teams
Veteran swing tackle returns on minimum deal as bridge option while team searches for long-term Havenstein replacement

key_losses.headline: 11-year Ram and last member of the St. Louis era retires, opening a significant starter hole at right tackle
Super Bowl LVI champion corner retires at 32, one of five CBs to depart — his exit helped trigger the McDuffie/Watson double acquisition
15-game starter in 2025 and team co-leader with 3 INTs departs as UFA, replaced and upgraded by the McDuffie-Watson tandem
Backup QB and fan favorite likely heads to Arizona, leaving Stetson Bennett as Stafford's only backup
Speed WR experiment ends after $10M guaranteed in 2025 yielded just 6 catches — departure barely felt given Rams' 13-personnel shift

draft_capital_note: The Rams' trade for McDuffie cost 2026 picks No. 29 (1st), 5th, and 6th, plus a 2027 3rd-rounder. The Rams retain the No. 13 overall pick (acquired from Atlanta in the 2025 draft trade). Their 2026 draft board is significantly thinner but the No. 13 pick gives them legitimate top-14 capital. With CB and S fully addressed, the top-of-draft focus likely shifts to OT, WR complement, EDGE depth, or IOL succession planning.
notes: New league year officially began March 11, 2026 at 4 PM ET. All deals with McDuffie (trade + extension), Watson, Curl, Higbee, Cardona, and Quessenberry were agreed during the legal tampering window (March 9-10) and officially execute today. Cobie Durant, Ahkello Witherspoon, Roger McCreary, Tutu Atwell, and Jimmy Garoppolo are confirmed UFAs with no reported re-signings as of this date. Durant and Garoppolo departures noted as unconfirmed final signings for new teams but classified as departed based on UFA status and lack of reported returns.
